hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'case "{}" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.mjs|*.cjs|*.json|*.yml|*.yaml|*.md) cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && pnpm exec prettier --write --log-level warn "{}" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac'

hooks.prompt:
You are auditing whether the typemove repo's CLAUDE.md needs updating after a Claude Code session.

Step 1 — Quick triage. Run `git status --short` and `git diff HEAD --stat` from the typemove repo root. If there are no file changes (nothing modified or staged), output exactly `SKIP: no changes` and STOP. Do not edit anything.

Step 2 — Relevance check. If changes exist, inspect the diff. typemove/CLAUDE.md should ONLY be updated when the session established something durable that a future Claude session would need to know:
  - New or changed pnpm scripts contributors must know about (build, build:all, test, lint, format, gen, gen:test in any package.json)
  - New package added under packages/ (e.g. support for a new Move chain) or a new top-level directory (examples/, scripts/, etc.)
  - Changed code-generation / type-generation workflow (codegen entrypoints, the typemove-aptos / typemove-sui / typemove-iota CLI flags, ABI fetch/regeneration steps)
  - Changed PR workflow, git aliases (git dev/sync), or commit conventions
  - New tech-stack version that changes how contributors build (Node, pnpm, TypeScript) or a new build/runtime requirement
  - Changed sentio-sdk integration or local-linking workflow
  - Changed publishing flow for the @typemove/* npm packages

Do NOT update for: routine bug fixes, one-off code changes, dependency version bumps that don't change workflow, internal refactors, regenerated builtin/ABI output, test-only changes, doc tweaks outside CLAUDE.md, or anything already captured.

SCOPE: typemove is a PUBLIC repository. Never add references to the private `sentio/` or `production/` repos, their structure, services, credentials, or internal URLs.

Step 3 — If an update is warranted, read typemove/CLAUDE.md and make a minimal, targeted edit matching its existing structure (headings, code-block style, callout style). Output a one-line summary of what you changed and why.

If no update is warranted, output exactly `SKIP: nothing CLAUDE.md-worthy` and STOP.

Hook input: $ARGUMENTS
hooks.statusMessage: Checking if typemove CLAUDE.md needs updating
